name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.09.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.09.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As  on  29  August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1092.87 lakh ha as compared to 1060.36 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 99.6 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As  on  29  August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1092.87 lakh ha as compared to 1060.36 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 99.6 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over North East India, Bihar and adjoining regions of East Uttar Pradesh and below normal over rest of the country.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over North East India, Bihar and adjoining regions of East Uttar Pradesh and below normal over rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 01 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 30%; (iii) Central India: 9%; and South Peninsula: 10%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 01 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 30%; (iii) Central India: 9%; and South Peninsula: 10%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very Heavy rainfall spell likely to continue over Northwest India during next 3 days  with  isolated  Extremely  heavy  rainfall  over  Himachal  Pradesh  during  1st  to  03 rd September and Uttarakhand on 01 st  September.
texts.text: Heavy to very Heavy rainfall spell likely to continue over Northwest India during next 3 days  with  isolated  Extremely  heavy  rainfall  over  Himachal  Pradesh  during  1st  to  03 rd September and Uttarakhand on 01 st  September.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  over  Jammu  during  1 st   to  02 nd   September  &  isolated Extremely Heavy rainfall on 02 nd  September, 2025.
texts.text: Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  over  Jammu  during  1 st   to  02 nd   September  &  isolated Extremely Heavy rainfall on 02 nd  September, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy  to very heavy rainfall likely to  continue  over  Konkan  &  Goa,  Madhya Maharashtra, Gujarat Region and Coastal Karnataka during next 7 days with possibility of Extremely heavy rainfall at isolated places over Gujarat during 04 th  06 th  September.
texts.text: Heavy  to very heavy rainfall likely to  continue  over  Konkan  &  Goa,  Madhya Maharashtra, Gujarat Region and Coastal Karnataka during next 7 days with possibility of Extremely heavy rainfall at isolated places over Gujarat during 04 th  06 th  September.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Scattered  to  Fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  northern,  western  India,  western peninsular and eastern India.
texts.text: Scattered  to  Fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  northern,  western  India,  western peninsular and eastern India.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  28 th   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 152.31 billion cubic meter, which is 83% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.7 percent of last year's storage and 121 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  28 th   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 152.31 billion cubic meter, which is 83% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.7 percent of last year's storage and 121 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice Black Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana  &  (Swarming  Caterpillar  and  Leaf  folder)  in Assam, for Cotton (Pink ball worm, Angular leaf spot/Bacterial leaf blight) in Haryana and for Green Gram (White Grub) in Rajasthan. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice Black Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana  &  (Swarming  Caterpillar  and  Leaf  folder)  in Assam, for Cotton (Pink ball worm, Angular leaf spot/Bacterial leaf blight) in Haryana and for Green Gram (White Grub) in Rajasthan. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (14 Aug - 27 Aug 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 37%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  27 th Aug 2025) is higher than LPA by 5%.
texts.text: During the week (14 Aug - 27 Aug 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 37%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  27 th Aug 2025) is higher than LPA by 5%.

texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  monsoon  trough  at  mean  sea  level  now  passes  through  Bikaner,  Gwalior,  Satna, Pendra Road, Puri and thence to eastwards to eastcentral Bay of Bengal.
texts.text: The  monsoon  trough  at  mean  sea  level  now  passes  through  Bikaner,  Gwalior,  Satna, Pendra Road, Puri and thence to eastwards to eastcentral Bay of Bengal.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of northwest & central India; near normal over most parts of east & northeast; below normal over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of northwest & central India; near normal over most parts of east & northeast; below normal over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 29 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West  Bengal, major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Telangana, Gujarat, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 29 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West  Bengal, major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Telangana, Gujarat, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar , in  pockets of eastern part  of  UP,  in  parts  of  Assam,  in  parts  of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  in  pockets  of southern parts of Odisha.
